greeting: The chainman has brass dust on his fingers and a measuring chain coiled neatly by his boot.
shrug: "Measure twice and ask once. I cannot help you there."
deflect: "Gantt dislikes idle talk in a working room."

entries.text: "Chainman. I hold the line while better coats decide where the line ought to be."
"Poles, chain, notes, angles. A survey is just truth with numbers until a man pays to change it."
"Every deed in Cumberland depends on a mark some tired man scratched into stone. Remember that."
"Brass does not flatter. Find enough benchmarks and the land will tell you who lied."
